metadata.title: Claude, Seeded.
metadata.reformat: center-cut, focus-aware; pantry/<bid>-916.* is the human replacement slot; -916 overrides honored

beats.narration_text: Bonjour — this is Liam, in for Bear. Ask Claude for art, and the first thing it writes is a manifesto. Not the code. Not the artwork. A manifesto for a named generative art movement. Only then does it write the algorithm. That is not a metaphor — it is literally the instruction file. Let me show you the strangest, smartest skill Anthropic ships.

props.command: Show me the algorithmic-art skill — how does it actually work, step by step?
output: Step 1: Claude founds a named art movement. Writes the manifesto.
Step 2: Claude writes the p5.js algorithm that lives up to it.
Output: one .md manifesto + one self-contained .html artifact.

beats.narration_text: A skill is a folder of instructions. Claude reads it before it works. This particular folder is called algorithmic-art, and it rewires the request 'draw me something' into a two-step pipeline. Step one: philosophy. Step two: code. The output is a dot-md manifesto and a single self-contained HTML artifact running p5.js inline. The terracotta arrow between the two phases is the interesting part — that handoff is where the invention happens. Phase one Claude writes a brief for phase two Claude.

beats.act: Act 4 — the code step / Art Blocks seed pattern
beats.narration_text: Phase two: p5.js. And the Art Blocks pattern — randomSeed and noiseSeed, both set to the same value. Same seed, identical output, forever. This is not a coincidence. This is a contract. The parameters must emerge from the philosophy — the skill's exact framing is 'how to express this philosophy through code?' — never chosen from a pattern menu. The philosophy writes the spec. The code implements it.

shot.new_visual_element: Onda code block — Art Blocks seed pattern

props.title: sketch.js — Art Blocks pattern

beats.narration_text: The algorithm is the plate. The seed is the print. Here is one algorithm, run with nine different seeds. Visibly the same species — visibly different individuals. Same branching patterns, same color logic, same field dynamics. Just different starting conditions. And seed 42, tile four — run it again with the same seed: pixel-identical. The skill calls this 'a series of prints from the same plate.' That is not a metaphor. That is the deliverable.

shot.new_visual_element: 3×3 seed grid — same algorithm, nine seeds

beats.act: Act 4 — fixed chrome vs variable algorithm
beats.narration_text: The artifact viewer your audience sees. Seed navigation, parameter sliders, color controls, action buttons — that sidebar is fixed. Anthropic's. The algorithm inside is free. Brand consistency outside, creative anarchy inside. And while the sidebar holds steady, watch what happens inside. The system swaps. Different movement, different parameters, completely different visual language — same chrome wrapping it. Which is the same law this video runs on: the Claude skin is fixed; the content is yours.

shot.new_visual_element: Artifact viewer mock — FIXED sidebar, VARIABLE algorithm

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Here is the prompt — read it with me. 'Create algorithmic art about something I love. First write me the manifesto for a new generative art movement, then build it as an interactive sketch — and hide one reference in the algorithm only I would catch.' That last clause is the skill working on you: you have to think about what is worth hiding before you even type. That is not a UX feature. That is the philosophy step, happening in your own head. Run this in your own Claude session. See what movement it founds. Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Create algorithmic art about something I love. First write me the manifesto for a new generative art movement, then build it as an interactive sketch — and hide one reference in the algorithm only I would catch.

beats.narration_text: That's the short version. The full video also covers Phase one: Claude must invent…, The skill lists four other… and Act three of the skill's… — watch Claude, Seeded. for the whole story. The link is right below.
